title: Experience
description: A record of technical decisions made in production, with measurable impact and attention to real product constraints.

items.context: A manual operational flow with high execution cost and a need for control across delivery, infrastructure, and evolution.
items.decision: Treat the automation as a complete internal product: React application, serverless APIs, and versioned infrastructure from the beginning.
items.impact: Reduced operational effort and total execution time, with a technical base ready for maintenance and auditability.
evidence: Responsive web application shaped around repeated operational use.
AWS Lambda backend with Python exposing secure flow APIs.
Terraform and Azure DevOps used for controlled, traceable delivery.

items.context: A production multi-tenant CRM, sustained by active legacy code and used by multiple clients without room for a broad rewrite.
items.decision: Attack bottlenecks through evidence: measure queries, refactor critical modules, and preserve compatibility where it protected the business.
items.impact: Reduced loading time from 82 seconds to under 2 seconds in production without breaking the existing ecosystem.
evidence: Used EXPLAIN to diagnose critical SQL queries.
Delivered with CodeIgniter, jQuery, and Bootstrap within legacy constraints.

items.context: An operation dependent on automated collection and file generation, with internal processes too slow for the pace of decision making.
items.decision: Separate what had to accelerate now from what had to prepare the architecture: direct legacy optimization and a cloud-native prototype in parallel.
items.impact: File generation moved from minutes to seconds while the next evolution gained a design oriented around scale and messaging.
evidence: Production system for web scraping collection supporting hiring decisions.
Replaced HTML with CSV to reduce processing time.
Cloud-native prototype with scalable architecture and messaging.
